title: bind mounted volumes with different user but matching groups fail
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have a /var/cache/ccache that is owned by root:ccache:

```
$ ls -ld /var/cache/ccache/
drwxrws---. 2 root ccache 6 Feb 19 06:44 /var/cache/ccache/
```

I am not root, but a proud member of the ccache group:

```
$ groups
ccache wheel mock stapusr stapsys stapdev wireshark avi docker
```

I have full access to /var/cache/ccache on the host, but on the guest, no access:

```
$ podman run -v /var/cache/ccache:/var/cache/ccache --rm docker.io/fedora:32 ls -ld /var/cache/ccache
drwxrws---. 2 nobody nobody 6 Feb 19 04:44 /var/cache/ccache
$ podman run -v /var/cache/ccache:/var/cache/ccache --rm docker.io/fedora:32 ls -l /var/cache/ccache
ls: cannot open directory '/var/cache/ccache': Permission denied
```

The directory is owned by nobody (expected) but also group-owned by nobody, although I do have access to it via the ccache group.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. dnf -y install ccache; sudo usermod $(id -un) -a -G ccache; newgrp ccache

2. podman run -v /var/cache/ccache:/var/cache/ccache --rm docker.io/fedora:32 ls -l /var/cache/ccache


**Describe the results you received:**

ls: cannot open directory '/var/cache/ccache': Permission denied


**Describe the results you expected:**

A big blinking green box flashing "Access Granted" in a large font.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.9.3
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  cpus: 12

  eventLogger: file
  hostname: avi
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1

      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.6.10-300.fc32.x86_64
  memFree: 2705891328
  memTotal: 33541308416
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.13-2.fc32.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.1-1.fc32.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.1
      commit: bbf27c5acd4356edb97fa639b4e15e0cd56a39d5
      libslirp: 4.2.0
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 2
  swapFree: 4713586688
  swapTotal: 16869486592
  uptime: 1079h 3m 18.17s (Approximately 44.96 days)

  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com

      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.9.1
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.1.0
        FUSE library version 3.9.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/avi/.local/share/containers/storage
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.9.3-1.fc32.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Container runtimes will, by default, drop all supplemental groups on entering the container (for security reasons).

The `crun` runtime, default on F32, allows disabling this behavior using the following flag to `podman run`: `--annotation run.oci.keep_original_groups=1`

(Though this does have some limitations of its own - we can't map the group into the user namespace because of permissions issues, so inside the container, files will appear to be owned by nobody:nobody)

Thanks, I confirm it works for me.

Why is dropping the groups helpful? I expect that if I have access to a file on the host, and I mount that file in a container, then I have access to the file in the container (regardless of _how_ I have access to the file in the host).

I believe the concern is that we'll leak a privileged group from the host (like `wheel`, or something similar) that will allow the container to, if it escapes, escalate permissions (or just access more of the system than it would otherwise). A lot of the security proposition of rootless containers is that, because they run only as your user and your group, they have a very limited ability to affect the system even if a vulnerability lets a process escape the container, and adding more groups does weaken that proposition.
Excellent answer https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/6774#issuecomment-649637610 Looks like the makings of a short blog.
